title: rhinanthus aristatus subsp. subalpinus
intro: Rhinanthus aristatus subsp. subalpinus, also known as the Alpine Rorippa or Alpine Yellow Bartsia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Rhinanthus aristatus subsp. subalpinus include its ability to soothe sore throats, alleviate congestion, and provide relief from asthma and bronchitis symptoms. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote respiratory health and well-being.
